 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.15.1","title":"TEST: Store/Placement/Repair Simulation + Bounds","description":"# TEST: Store/Placement/Repair Simulation + Bounds

## Goal
Make offline resilience measurable and bounded. Prove that the store layer maintains availability guarantees.

## Test Categories

### Object Store Tests
- [ ] Object write produces correct ObjectId (keyed derivation)
- [ ] Object read returns identical bytes
- [ ] Missing object returns appropriate error
- [ ] Object metadata (zone, schema, retention) is preserved
- [ ] Object reference counting works correctly
- [ ] Object deletion respects GC roots

### Symbol Store Tests
- [ ] Symbols stored with correct ESI mapping
- [ ] Symbol retrieval by ESI works
- [ ] Symbol availability tracking is accurate
- [ ] Symbol eviction respects retention class
- [ ] Quarantine zone isolation

### Placement Policy Tests
- [ ] Coverage evaluation correctness (basis points calculation)
- [ ] min_coverage threshold enforcement
- [ ] max_concentration limit enforcement (no single-source)
- [ ] Geographic diversity when node location available
- [ ] Placement decisions are deterministic (same inputs → same placement)

### Background Repair Loop Tests
- [ ] Repair loop improves coverage over time (measurable)
- [ ] Repair respects bandwidth budget
- [ ] Repair prioritizes objects below threshold
- [ ] Repair actions produce audit events
- [ ] Repair loop is idempotent (no duplicate work)

### Retention/GC Tests
- [ ] GC roots (ZoneCheckpoint) prevent premature eviction
- [ ] Retention class ordering: Required > Pinned > Cached > Ephemeral
- [ ] Ephemeral objects evicted first under pressure
- [ ] GC produces audit events
- [ ] No orphaned symbols after GC

### ChunkedObjectManifest Tests
- [ ] Large object → manifest + chunks
- [ ] Chunk placement distributed correctly
- [ ] Manifest references valid chunk ObjectIds
- [ ] Reassembly produces original object
- [ ] Partial chunk availability tracked correctly

### Quarantine Tests
- [ ] Quarantine eviction order matches policy (by age, by risk)
- [ ] Quarantined objects isolated from normal storage
- [ ] Quarantine entry produces audit event
- [ ] Quarantine promotion/demotion paths

## Simulation Framework
```rust
#[test]
fn test_repair_improves_coverage() {
    let harness = StoreSimulation::new()
        .with_nodes(5)
        .with_failure_rate(0.1);
    
    harness.store_object(test_object, min_coverage: 5000);
    harness.fail_node(2);
    
    assert!(harness.coverage() < 5000);
    harness.run_repair_loop(Duration::from_secs(10));
    assert!(harness.coverage() >= 5000);
}
```

## Golden Vectors
- Object storage: content → ObjectId mapping
- Placement decisions: node set → placement result
- Coverage calculation: symbol distribution → basis points

## Logging Requirements
All store tests MUST emit structured JSON:
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_repair_improves_coverage",
  "module": "fcp-store",
  "phase": "execute|verify",
  "operation": "write|read|repair|gc|placement",
  "object_id": "...",
  "object_size": 12345,
  "symbol_count": 13,
  "coverage_bps": 7500,
  "nodes_holding": ["node-0", "node-2", "node-4"],
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "duration_us": 1234
}
```

### For Repair Actions
```json
{
  "repair_action": "replicate",
  "object_id": "...",
  "source_node": "node-0",
  "target_node": "node-3",
  "coverage_before_bps": 4000,
  "coverage_after_bps": 6000,
  "reason_code": "BELOW_THRESHOLD"
}
```

### For GC Events
```json
{
  "gc_action": "evict",
  "object_id": "...",
  "retention_class": "Ephemeral",
  "reason": "SPACE_PRESSURE",
  "gc_root_checked": true
}
```

## Determinism Requirements
- Simulation tests MUST use seeded RNG
- All tests MUST be reproducible from seed
- No flaky tests allowed

## Acceptance Criteria
- Simulation tests run deterministically
- Repair loop measurably improves coverage
- GC respects retention and roots
- All operations produce structured logs
- Performance bounds verified","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T01:53:15.876297693-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T21:22:11.03409037-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-15T21:22:11.03409037-05:00","close_reason":"Added GC symbol pruning + test","labels":["fcp2","mvp","storage","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.15.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.15","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-15T01:53:15.877470052-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.15.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:52:31.665556957-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.16","title":"[FCP2] Tailscale Integration: MeshIdentity, NodeKeyAttestation, Peer Discovery, ACL Tag Mapping","description":"# [FCP2] Tailscale Integration: MeshIdentity, NodeKeyAttestation, Peer Discovery, ACL Tag Mapping

## Goal
Abstract Tailscale as the authenticated mesh substrate.

## Required behaviors
### MeshIdentity model
MeshIdentity MUST include:
- tailscale node_id
- hostname
- IPs
- ACL tags
- owner_pubkey anchor
- node signing/encryption/issuance pubkeys + kids
- owner-signed NodeKeyAttestation binding `node_id ↔ keys ↔ tags` (and optional DevicePostureAttestation)

### Peer discovery
Provide peer discovery and a stable view of online peers (mockable for tests).

Implementation guidance (non-binding):
- use Tailscale LocalAPI (`/localapi/v0/status`, `/localapi/v0/whois`) behind an interface

### Zone ↔ tag mapping + ACL generation
- ZoneId maps to a Tailscale tag: `tag:fcp-<suffix>`.
- Port-gating ACL generation is a defense-in-depth feature:
  - zone members can reach only zone ports (symbol_port/control_port)
  - policy engine still enforces the true integrity/confidentiality lattice

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests for tag<->zone mapping.
- Mockable interface so tests don't require a real tailnet.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.17","title":"[FCP2] MeshNode: Routing, Gossip, Admission Control, Leases, Device-Aware Execution","description":"# [FCP2] MeshNode: Routing, Gossip, Admission Control, Leases, Device-Aware Execution

## Goal
Implement the core runtime node that turns the spec into behavior:
- peer discovery (via Tailscale abstraction)
- session establishment + replay policy
- symbol routing + multipath delivery
- gossip / announcements + reconciliation
- admission control + anti-amplification
- enforcement: zones/caps/taint/approvals/revocation/checkpoint/policy
- distributed coordination: leases
- connector state coordination hooks (singleton_writer)
- device-aware execution planner + delegation

## Why Users Care
MeshNode is where "mechanical security" becomes real:
- It is the enforcement boundary between untrusted inputs and privileged side effects.
- It produces DecisionReceipts and audit events, enabling `fcp explain` and incident response.
- It makes offline availability measurable (coverage) and repairable.

## Recommended Runtime Stack (2025-2026)

| Component | Crate | Version | Notes |
|-----------|-------|---------|-------|
| Async runtime | `tokio` | 1.44+ | io-uring on Linux |
| Networking | `quinn` | 0.12+ | QUIC transport |
| Gossip | `crdt-rs` / custom | - | CRDT-based state sync |
| Hash ring | `hashring` | 0.3+ | Consistent hashing |
| Timer wheel | `tokio-util` | 0.7+ | Efficient timeouts |
| Metrics | `metrics` | 0.24+ | Prometheus/OTEL |
| Tracing | `tracing` | 0.2+ | Distributed tracing |

## Normative Behaviors (Spec-Aligned)

### Default Deny Enforcement Spine
No operation executes unless it passes, in order:
1. canonical decode + schema validation
2. capability token verification (including checkpoint freshness + holder proof)
3. revocation freshness enforcement (per policy tier)
4. taint/approval enforcement (elevation/declassification)
5. zone policy ceilings + connector manifest constraints
6. (when required) execution lease acquisition/validation

Every denial MUST emit a DecisionReceipt with stable `reason_code` and evidence ObjectIds.

```rust
/// Enforcement result with full provenance
pub struct EnforcementResult {
    pub decision: Decision,
    pub reason_code: ReasonCode,
    pub evidence: Vec<ObjectId>,
    pub trace_id: TraceId,
    pub latency: Duration,
}

pub enum Decision {
    Allow { receipt: OperationReceipt },
    Deny { receipt: DecisionReceipt },
}
```

### Transport Priority + Multipath Delivery
Transport path selection is user-visible and policy-controlled.
- Priority order: Direct (LAN) → Mesh NAT traversal → DERP → Funnel
- ZoneTransportPolicy controls DERP/Funnel availability (deny-by-default for high-trust zones)
- Multipath symbol delivery MAY use multiple paths concurrently when allowed

```rust
/// Transport selection with locality scoring
pub struct TransportSelector {
    pub direct: Option<DirectPath>,
    pub mesh: Option<MeshPath>,
    pub derp: Option<DerpPath>,
    pub funnel: Option<FunnelPath>,
}

impl TransportSelector {
    pub fn best_path(&self, policy: &ZoneTransportPolicy) -> Option<&dyn Transport> {
        // Priority-ordered selection respecting policy
    }
}
```

### Admission Control + DoS Resistance (NORMATIVE)
MeshNodes MUST implement admission control for:
- per-peer inbound bytes/symbols
- failed decrypt/MAC counters
- bounded concurrent decodes + decode CPU budgets
- bounded gossip reconciliation work

```rust
/// Admission control state per peer
pub struct PeerAdmission {
    pub inbound_bytes: RateLimiter,      // Token bucket
    pub symbols_per_second: RateLimiter,
    pub failed_mac_count: AtomicU32,     // Quarantine trigger
    pub concurrent_decodes: Semaphore,
    pub quarantined_until: Option<Instant>,
}
```

Anti-amplification rule (NORMATIVE):
- do not send more than `N` symbols in response to a request unless the requester is authenticated AND presents bounded proof-of-need (e.g., missing-hint).

### Gossip Protocol (CRDT-Based)

```rust
/// Gossip message types
pub enum GossipMessage {
    /// Announce object availability (Bloom filter)
    ObjectAnnounce { filter: BloomFilter, version: u64 },
    /// Request reconciliation (XOR filter diff)
    SyncRequest { filter: XorFilter },
    /// CRDT state merge
    StateMerge { crdt: MeshStateCrdt },
}

/// CRDT for mesh state convergence
pub struct MeshStateCrdt {
    pub checkpoint_heads: LWWRegister<ZoneCheckpoint>,
    pub revocation_heads: LWWRegister<RevocationHead>,
    pub peer_liveness: GCounter,
}
```

### Control-Plane Fallback over FCPS (Spec Surface)
In degraded/offline sync scenarios, control-plane objects may be encoded to symbols and transported in FCPS frames with `FrameFlags::CONTROL_PLANE`.
MeshNode owns the semantics here (routing + reconstruction + retention enforcement).

### Execution Planner (Device-Aware)
MeshNode chooses where to execute operations based on:
- device profiles (cpu/mem/battery)
- connector availability + version requirements
- symbol locality scoring + DERP penalty
- (full profile) secret reconstruction cost

```rust
/// Device profile for execution planning
pub struct DeviceProfile {
    pub cpu_class: CpuClass,          // mobile | desktop | server
    pub memory_mb: u32,
    pub battery_powered: bool,
    pub battery_percent: Option<u8>,
    pub connector_versions: HashMap<ConnectorId, Version>,
    pub symbol_locality: f64,         // 0.0-1.0 (local availability)
}

pub enum ExecutionDecision {
    ExecuteLocal,
    DelegateToNode { node_id: NodeId, reason: String },
    WaitForSymbols { missing: Vec<SymbolId> },
    Reject { reason: ReasonCode },
}
```

## Performance Optimizations

### io_uring Integration (Linux)
```rust
#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
pub async fn symbol_batch_read(store: &SymbolStore, ids: &[SymbolId]) -> Vec<Symbol> {
    // Use io_uring for batched reads when available
    tokio_uring::fs::read_batch(...)
}
```

### Zero-Copy Symbol Handling
```rust
/// Zero-copy symbol buffer
pub struct SymbolBuffer {
    data: bytes::Bytes,  // Reference-counted, zero-copy
    offset: usize,
}
```

## Observability

```rust
#[tracing::instrument(
    level = "info",
    skip(symbols),
    fields(
        operation = %operation_id,
        zone = %zone_id,
        peer = %peer_id,
        trace_id = %trace_context.trace_id,
    )
)]
async fn route_symbols(&self, symbols: Vec<Symbol>) -> Result<(), RoutingError> {
    metrics::counter\!("mesh.symbols.routed").increment(symbols.len() as u64);
    // ...
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Integration tests (simulated mesh) for:
  - sessions + replay
  - routing + reconstruction
  - allow path (receipt + audit)
  - deny path (DecisionReceipt with evidence)
  - admission control rejecting floods
- Benchmarks for local invoke overhead
- CRDT gossip convergence under partition
- Device-aware execution delegation tests

## Notes
This issue is intentionally an umbrella; complex sub-behaviors are tracked as child beads (routing/multipath, admission+quarantine, gossip, execution planner, FCPS control-plane fallback).

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.17.1","title":"TEST: MeshNode Integration Scenarios (Routing + Enforcement)","description":"# TEST: MeshNode Integration Scenarios (Routing + Enforcement)


## Goal
Validate MeshNode routing/enforcement end-to-end in tests (multi-node), including admission control, lease behavior, and policy gating, with structured logs.

## Scope
Integration tests for MeshNode routing, admission control, and policy enforcement.

## Test Categories

### Routing
- [ ] Symbol routing to correct nodes based on placement
- [ ] Control-plane message routing
- [ ] Multi-hop routing when direct path unavailable
- [ ] Routing table updates on topology changes
- [ ] Load balancing across equivalent paths

### Admission Control
- [ ] Valid requests admitted
- [ ] Requests without session rejected
- [ ] Rate-limited requests rejected with backpressure
- [ ] Quarantined nodes blocked
- [ ] Anti-amplification limits enforced

### Policy Enforcement
- [ ] Zone boundary enforcement in routing
- [ ] Capability verification before forwarding
- [ ] Revocation checks on forwarded requests
- [ ] Taint labels propagated correctly

### Gossip Integration
- [ ] Object availability propagates
- [ ] Reconciliation completes after partition
- [ ] Stale gossip is rejected
- [ ] Gossip rate limiting

### Lease Coordination
- [ ] Lease acquisition via HRW
- [ ] Lease renewal works
- [ ] Lease transfer on node failure
- [ ] Lease conflicts detected

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/mesh_node/`
- `routing_table.json` - Expected routing decisions
- `admission_scenarios.json` - Allow/deny test cases
- `gossip_messages.cbor` - Gossip message examples

## Test Infrastructure
- Deterministic mesh harness (3+ nodes)
- Network fault injection
- Clock control for timeouts
- Structured log collection

## Logging Requirements
All tests MUST emit structured JSON:
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_symbol_routing",
  "module": "fcp-mesh",
  "phase": "execute",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "nodes": ["node-0", "node-1", "node-2"],
  "source_node": "node-0",
  "target_node": "node-2",
  "hops": 1,
  "result": "pass"
}
```

### For Admission Tests
```json
{
  "request_type": "invoke",
  "admission_decision": "allow|deny",
  "deny_reason": "FCP-4001",
  "rate_limit_remaining": 95
}
```

### For Gossip Tests
```json
{
  "gossip_type": "object_available",
  "object_id": "...",
  "propagation_time_ms": 50,
  "nodes_reached": 3
}
```

## CI Integration
```bash
cargo test -p fcp-mesh --test mesh_integration -- --nocapture --test-threads=1
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All routing scenarios tested
- Admission control is complete
- Gossip converges correctly
- Structured logs produced

+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.17.1","title":"TEST: MeshNode Integration Scenarios (Routing + Enforcement)","description":"# TEST: MeshNode Integration Scenarios (Routing + Enforcement)


## Goal
Validate MeshNode routing/enforcement end-to-end in tests (multi-node), including admission control, lease behavior, and policy gating, with structured logs.

## Scope
Integration tests for MeshNode routing, admission control, and policy enforcement.

## Test Categories

### Routing
- [ ] Symbol routing to correct nodes based on placement
- [ ] Control-plane message routing
- [ ] Multi-hop routing when direct path unavailable
- [ ] Routing table updates on topology changes
- [ ] Load balancing across equivalent paths

### Admission Control
- [ ] Valid requests admitted
- [ ] Requests without session rejected
- [ ] Rate-limited requests rejected with backpressure
- [ ] Quarantined nodes blocked
- [ ] Anti-amplification limits enforced

### Policy Enforcement
- [ ] Zone boundary enforcement in routing
- [ ] Capability verification before forwarding
- [ ] Revocation checks on forwarded requests
- [ ] Taint labels propagated correctly

### Gossip Integration
- [ ] Object availability propagates
- [ ] Reconciliation completes after partition
- [ ] Stale gossip is rejected
- [ ] Gossip rate limiting

### Lease Coordination
- [ ] Lease acquisition via HRW
- [ ] Lease renewal works
- [ ] Lease transfer on node failure
- [ ] Lease conflicts detected

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/mesh_node/`
- `routing_table.json` - Expected routing decisions
- `admission_scenarios.json` - Allow/deny test cases
- `gossip_messages.cbor` - Gossip message examples

## Test Infrastructure
- Deterministic mesh harness (3+ nodes)
- Network fault injection
- Clock control for timeouts
- Structured log collection

## Logging Requirements
All tests MUST emit structured JSON:
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_symbol_routing",
  "module": "fcp-mesh",
  "phase": "execute",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "nodes": ["node-0", "node-1", "node-2"],
  "source_node": "node-0",
  "target_node": "node-2",
  "hops": 1,
  "result": "pass"
}
```

### For Admission Tests
```json
{
  "request_type": "invoke",
  "admission_decision": "allow|deny",
  "deny_reason": "FCP-4001",
  "rate_limit_remaining": 95
}
```

### For Gossip Tests
```json
{
  "gossip_type": "object_available",
  "object_id": "...",
  "propagation_time_ms": 50,
  "nodes_reached": 3
}
```

## CI Integration
```bash
cargo test -p fcp-mesh --test mesh_integration -- --nocapture --test-threads=1
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All routing scenarios tested
- Admission control is complete
- Gossip converges correctly
- Structured logs produced

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.17.2","title":"[FCP2-MESH] Transport Priority + Multipath Delivery Engine","description":"# [FCP2-MESH] Transport Priority + Multipath Delivery Engine

## Goal
Implement spec-aligned **path selection** and **multipath symbol delivery**.

## Requirements
- Transport priority order (default):
  1. Tailscale Direct (same LAN)
  2. Tailscale Mesh (NAT traversal)
  3. Tailscale DERP Relay (policy-controlled per zone)
  4. Tailscale Funnel/public ingress (policy-controlled; low-trust zones only by default)

- ZoneTransportPolicy MUST be enforced:
  - high-trust zones can forbid DERP/Funnel
  - low-trust zones can allow Funnel with explicit constraints

- Multipath symbol delivery:
  - when multiple paths are available and allowed by policy, MeshNode can send/receive symbols on multiple paths concurrently
  - load balancing strategy must be deterministic (so behavior is testable and reproducible)

## Deliverables
- A routing/path-selection module that:
  - ranks candidate paths based on priority + penalties (e.g., DERP penalty)
  - emits structured logs of chosen path and policy decision
- Multipath sender/receiver plumbing:
  - consistent session establishment per peer
  - bounded concurrency and backpressure

## Tests
- Deterministic tests for path ranking given a fixed peer topology.
- Policy tests: DERP forbidden in z:owner-like zones yields denial reason codes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- In the E2E harness, symbol transfer works over each supported path type.
- Logs clearly show which path was used and why.

"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.17.3","title":"[FCP2-MESH] Admission Control + Anti-Amplification + Quarantine Enforcement","description":"# [FCP2-MESH] Admission Control + Anti-Amplification + Quarantine Enforcement

## Goal
Implement the **DoS resistance** and **admission-control** requirements for the mesh layer.

This is non-negotiable: without bounded budgets, the mesh becomes the largest attack surface.

## Normative requirements (spec-aligned)
MeshNodes MUST implement admission control for:
- per-peer inbound bytes/symbols
- failed decrypt/MAC counters
- bounded concurrent decodes
- bounded gossip reconciliation work

### PeerBudget (defaults are policy-tunable)
- `max_bytes_per_min` (default 64MB/min)
- `max_symbols_per_min` (default 200k/min)
- `max_failed_auth_per_min` (default 100/min)
- `max_inflight_decodes` (default 32)
- `max_decode_cpu_ms_per_min` (default 5000ms/min)

### Anti-amplification rule (NORMATIVE)
MeshNodes MUST NOT send more than `N` symbols in response to a request unless the requester:
1. is authenticated (session MAC or node signature), AND
2. presents bounded proof-of-need (e.g., DecodeStatus.missing_hint) or comparable evidence.

### Quarantine enforcement (owned jointly with Stores)
- Unknown/unreferenced ObjectIds MUST be quarantined by default (bounded TTL + quotas).
- Quarantined objects MUST NOT be inserted into global gossip state.
- Promotion rules are strict (reachability from ZoneCheckpoint / authenticated bounded request / explicit pin) and MUST require schema validation.

## Deliverables
- A single admission-control module used consistently by:
  - FCPS datagram handling
  - symbol_request handling
  - gossip reconciliation
  - decode/reconstruction scheduling
- Explicit reason codes + structured logs for rejections.

## Tests
- Property tests: budgets cannot go negative; counters are monotonic and bounded.
- Adversarial tests:
  - flood of invalid MACs triggers peer ban/throttle
  - decode CPU DoS corpus stays within time bounds
  - quarantine quotas enforce eviction

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E harness can simulate malicious peers and show bounded impact.
- Logs clearly explain admission-control denials.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.17.4","title":"[FCP2-MESH] SymbolRequest + Targeted Repair (DecodeStatus/SymbolAck)","description":"# [FCP2-MESH] SymbolRequest + Targeted Repair (DecodeStatus/SymbolAck)

## Goal
Make symbol transfer efficient and DoS-resistant by implementing:
- bounded SymbolRequest handling
- flow-control feedback via DecodeStatus
- stop conditions via SymbolAck
- targeted repair (send missing symbols, not floods)

## Normative requirements (spec-aligned)
### SymbolRequest bounding
- A requester MUST bound requests (max_symbols and/or missing-hint proof-of-need).
- A responder MUST NOT send more than `max_symbols` symbols in response.
- Unauthenticated requests MUST be capped tighter (default max_symbols_unauthenticated = 32) unless zone policy allows.
- Accounting rule: SymbolRequest processing counts against PeerBudget (bytes + CPU + inflight decodes).

### DecodeStatus (control-plane object)
- Receiver reports how many unique symbols received vs required, optionally with a compact missing-hint.
- Used for targeted repair and anti-amplification compliance.

### SymbolAck (control-plane object)
- Receiver can tell sender to stop: object reconstructed.

## Deliverables
- Canonical object schemas for DecodeStatus + SymbolAck (retention: Ephemeral).
- MeshNode handlers that:
  - validate bounds and enforce admission control
  - use missing-hint when present for targeted repair
  - stop sending on SymbolAck
  - emit structured logs/metrics for repair actions

## Tests
- Property tests: bounded requests never trigger unbounded sends.
- Adversarial tests: spoofed SymbolRequests can’t amplify traffic.
- Integration tests in E2E harness: reconstruction converges faster with targeted repair.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Multi-node simulation shows stable convergence under loss without budget blowups.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8qx","title":"fcp.reddit: Moderation Actions","description":"# fcp.reddit: Moderation Actions (FCP2)

## Goal
Expose a tightly-controlled moderation surface for moderators:
- approve/remove content
- manage mod queue
- ban/mute users (optional)

These actions are high-blast-radius and must be policy + approval gated.

## Scope
- List modqueue/reports.
- Approve/remove a post/comment.
- Optional: ban user (requires strict policy).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.mod.read`
- `reddit.mod.write` (dangerous)
- Optional: `reddit.mod.ban` (critical)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- For any moderation write:
  - require ApprovalToken when policy dictates (recommended default)
  - receipts + audit events include thing_id + action + reason (sanitized)
- Avoid logging raw user content.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.mod.queue.list`
  - `reddit.mod.approve`
  - `reddit.mod.remove`
  - Optional: `reddit.mod.ban_user`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - approval gating
  - audit/receipt emission
  - forbidden/permission errors

## Acceptance criteria
- Moderation actions are mechanically authorized and auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8tv","title":"fcp.kubernetes: Zone architecture for prod safety (dev/staging/prod isolation)","description":"# fcp.kubernetes: Zone architecture for prod safety (dev/staging/prod isolation) (FCP2)

## Goal
Define a safe-by-default zone strategy for Kubernetes operations, especially production.

Important: the connector instance is **single-zone bound**. “Dev/staging/prod isolation” is achieved by deploying separate connector instances bound to separate zones and pinned to separate cluster API hosts.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Scope
- Recommended zone naming patterns (policy-owned):
  - `z:work:k8s:dev:<cluster>`
  - `z:work:k8s:staging:<cluster>`
  - `z:work:k8s:prod:<cluster>`
- Capability tiering guidance:
  - readonly vs deploy vs exec
  - forbid deletes in prod by default
- Approval guidance:
  - prod deploy and exec should require ApprovalToken

## Deliverables
- Documented default policy expectations (reason codes + deny-by-default rules) referenced by connector operations.
- Clear mapping between zone policy and Kubernetes RBAC contexts.

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Policy-oriented tests (mock-only) verifying:
  - prod zone denies mutations by default
  - approval gating required for exec/deploy

## Acceptance criteria
- A user can set up three connector instances and be mechanically confident prod is protected.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-8v34","title":"TEST: Enrollment Unit Tests (DeviceEnrollment, NodeKeyAttestation, Removal Workflow)","description":"# TEST: Enrollment Unit Tests

## Goal
Prove that device enrollment and removal work correctly and securely.

## Test Categories

### DeviceEnrollment Object
- [ ] Valid enrollment parses correctly
- [ ] Owner signature verifies
- [ ] Tailscale node_id binding correct
- [ ] Authorized tags match
- [ ] Expiry (if present) enforced
- [ ] Malformed enrollment rejected

### NodeKeyAttestation
- [ ] Attestation binds node_id to pubkeys
- [ ] signing_pubkey correctly attested
- [ ] encryption_pubkey correctly attested
- [ ] issuance_pubkey correctly attested
- [ ] KID mappings correct
- [ ] Owner signature verifies
- [ ] Attestation expiry enforced

### Enrollment Flow
- [ ] New node can enroll with valid enrollment object
- [ ] Enrolled node receives zone keys (ZoneKeyManifest)
- [ ] Enrolled node can participate in sessions
- [ ] Enrolled node appears in peer discovery

### Key Rotation
- [ ] Node can rotate its keys
- [ ] New attestation replaces old
- [ ] Old KIDs become invalid
- [ ] Sessions using old keys gracefully terminated

### Device Removal
- [ ] Removal revokes enrollment object
- [ ] Removal revokes attestation
- [ ] Removal revokes issuer keys
- [ ] Tokens from removed node rejected
- [ ] Zone keys rotated after removal
- [ ] Removed node cannot participate

### Attack Scenarios (Adversarial)
- [ ] Replay old enrollment: rejected (revoked or expired)
- [ ] Forge enrollment: invalid owner signature rejected
- [ ] Use removed node keys: rejected after revocation propagates
- [ ] Impersonate node_id: attestation signature check fails

## Golden Vectors
- Valid DeviceEnrollment bytes
- Valid NodeKeyAttestation bytes
- Removal workflow sequence

## Integration Tests
- [ ] Full enrollment → operation → removal cycle
- [ ] Multi-node enrollment
- [ ] Concurrent enrollment attempts

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "node_id": "...",
  "operation": "enroll|attest|rotate|remove",
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "reason_code": "..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Enrollment flow works end-to-end
- Removal flow invalidates all node authority
- No stale credentials accepted after removal

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8w4","title":"fcp.kubernetes: Exec into pods for debugging and maintenance","description":"# fcp.kubernetes: Exec into pods for debugging and maintenance (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide a controlled exec surface for debugging:
- run a constrained command in a container

Exec is **high risk** (arbitrary code execution in prod). Must be approval gated and heavily audited.

## Scope
- Start an exec session with:
  - explicit namespace/pod/container
  - explicit command allowlist (policy-owned)
  - explicit timeout
- Capture session transcript metadata (not raw output by default).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `k8s.exec` (critical)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Approval gating recommended default for exec.
- Audit:
  - emit OperationReceipt + AuditEvent including command hash + targets
  - never log secrets/output by default

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `k8s.exec.start`
  - `k8s.exec.status`
  - `k8s.exec.cancel`
- Safety layer:
  - deny interactive shells by default
  - deny dangerous commands unless explicitly allowed

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - allowlist enforcement
  - approval gating
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Exec cannot run without explicit authorization and is fully auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8yxg","title":"fcp.arxiv: Research Literature and Paper Access Connector","description":"# fcp.arxiv - Research Literature and Paper Access Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Plan and implement an FCP2-compliant arXiv connector for literature search and paper retrieval (metadata + PDF) under safe NetworkConstraints and input validation.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 78/100** — Specialized Tier 3 connector (Research family).

**Why Critical**: ArXiv is the **primary preprint server for STEM research**, hosting 2M+ papers. Essential for research agents, AI/ML practitioners, and anyone needing access to cutting-edge scientific literature. Enables autonomous research workflows.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Specialized Investment (Research + Domain Knowledge)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 3 Enterprise (weeks 9-12)

### Semantic Scholar Comparison
- **ArXiv**: Raw preprints, full PDF access, direct source
- **Semantic Scholar**: Citation graphs, abstracts, AI-enhanced search
- Both needed for comprehensive research access

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Search & Discovery
- Query papers by topic, author, date
- Category-based filtering (cs.AI, cs.LG, etc.)
- Full-text search via abstract
- Citation tracking

### Content Access
- Download PDFs
- Access paper metadata
- Get LaTeX source (when available)
- Version history

### Research Workflows
- Literature review automation
- Citation network exploration
- Trend analysis across time
- New paper alerts

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Find recent papers on transformer architectures"
"Download papers cited by this one"
"What's new in cs.AI this week?"
"Find all papers by this author"
"Summarize the abstract of this paper"
"Track citations for these foundational papers"
"Compare methods across these papers"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### CASS (Context-Aware Semantic Search)
**Research knowledge base:**
- Index paper abstracts
- Semantic search over literature
- Build research corpus

### Vector DB Integration
- Paper embeddings for similarity search
- Find related work automatically
- Cluster papers by topic

### AI Provider Integration
- Summarize papers with LLMs
- Extract key findings
- Generate literature reviews

### Agent Mail Integration
- Research findings → agent threads
- Collaborative literature review
- Share paper recommendations

### Notion/Airtable Integration
- Track reading lists
- Annotate papers
- Organize research projects

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
ArXiv provides OAI-PMH and REST-like access:

- **Search API**: `export.arxiv.org/api/query`
- **OAI-PMH**: Metadata harvesting
- **PDF Access**: Direct download via arxiv.org

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `quick-xml` — Atom/XML parsing
- `url` — URL construction
- `regex` — ArXiv ID parsing

### ArXiv ID Format
```
Old: hep-th/9901001
New: 2301.12345
Version: 2301.12345v2
```

### Response Format (Atom)
```xml
<entry>
  <id>http://arxiv.org/abs/2301.12345v1</id>
  <title>Paper Title</title>
  <summary>Abstract text...</summary>
  <author><name>Author Name</name></author>
  <category term="cs.AI" scheme="http://arxiv.org/schemas/atom"/>
  <published>2023-01-25T00:00:00Z</published>
</entry>
```

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:research:arxiv:full` | Full access | Search and download |
| `z:research:arxiv:search` | Search only | Metadata access |
| `z:research:arxiv:category:<cat>` | Category-scoped | e.g., cs.AI only |

### Data Considerations
- Papers are public — low sensitivity
- Download volume should be respectful
- Bulk access has rate limits

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `operational` — REST API operations

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `export.arxiv.org:443` (API)
- `arxiv.org:443` (PDF download)

### Credential Injection
- No authentication required
- Rate limiting is IP-based
- Respect robots.txt

---

## Capability Model

### Read Operations
- `arxiv.search` — Query papers
- `arxiv.metadata` — Get paper details
- `arxiv.pdf.download` — Download PDF
- `arxiv.source.download` — Download LaTeX source

### No Write Operations
ArXiv is read-only for consumers.

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Search Papers
```
GET /api/query?
  search_query=all:transformer+AND+cat:cs.AI
  &start=0
  &max_results=10
  &sortBy=submittedDate
  &sortOrder=descending
```

### Get Paper by ID
```
GET /api/query?id_list=2301.12345
```

### Download PDF
```
GET https://arxiv.org/pdf/2301.12345.pdf
```

### Category Codes
| Code | Field |
|------|-------|
| cs.AI | Artificial Intelligence |
| cs.LG | Machine Learning |
| cs.CL | Computation and Language (NLP) |
| cs.CV | Computer Vision |
| stat.ML | Statistics - ML |
| math.* | Mathematics |
| physics.* | Physics |

---

## Research Workflow Patterns

### Literature Review
1. Search for topic keywords
2. Retrieve top N papers
3. Extract abstracts
4. Use LLM to summarize findings
5. Identify citation chains
6. Build reading list

### New Paper Monitoring
1. Query recent submissions by category
2. Filter by keywords/authors
3. Generate daily digest
4. Track citation counts over time

### Citation Network Analysis
1. Start with seed papers
2. Extract references
3. Recursively fetch cited papers
4. Build citation graph
5. Identify influential works

---

## Rate Limiting

### ArXiv Policies
- 1 request per 3 seconds recommended
- Bulk downloads require coordination
- Respect robots.txt
- Consider using data dumps for large-scale analysis

### Implementation
```rust
// Rate limiter: max 20 requests per minute
let limiter = RateLimiter::new(20, Duration::from_secs(60));
```

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Search query construction
- Atom feed parsing
- ArXiv ID validation
- Category handling

### Integration Tests
- wiremock-based API simulation
- Pagination testing
- Multi-paper fetch scenarios

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints
- Rate limit enforcement
- Audit logging

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real ArXiv calls in unit tests
- Search queries functional
- PDF download works
- Atom response parsing correct
- Rate limiting implemented
- Category filtering works

## Success Criteria
- Connector epic is fully decomposed into implementable beads (manifest/cap map, provisioning, operations, tests, E2E compliance).
- All child beads are implemented and closed, and the connector passes the mechanical compliance runner + its E2E scenarios with structured JSON logs.
- Mechanical guarantees hold: single-zone binding, default-deny capabilities, and NetworkConstraints enforcement; no secret/PII leakage in logs.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8yxg.1","title":"fcp.arxiv: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"# fcp.arxiv: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)

## Goal
Define the arXiv connector’s **mechanical manifest contract** under FCP2.

## Required contents
- Manifest archetypes (closed set):
  - `knowledge` (search/retrieve papers)
  - `operational` (monitors/alerts configuration)
  - `streaming` (polling-backed event stream for new papers)
- Operation list + schema IDs.
- Capability families + per-operation required caps.
- NetworkConstraints:
  - pin arXiv API hosts
  - pin PDF download hosts
  - enforce strict rate limits to avoid bans
- State model declaration:
  - monitor cursors/checkpoints for polling
  - cached metadata rules (must be bounded)

## Acceptance criteria
- Another engineer can implement `--manifest` from this bead alone.
